The sample was composed of two transparent, colorless pieces: one prismatic, 1.8 cm x 1.8 cm x 2 cm, and weighing about 2 g, the other a 5 mm x 5 mm x 5 mm cleavage fragment weighing 0.65 g. No impurities were detected in hand sample or microscopically. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.coarse.aragon1.spectrum.txt

The sample consisted of transparent euhedral crystals. The largest was about 2 cm x 2 cm. This was obviously pure optical quality calcite. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.coarse.calcit1.spectrum.txt

No solid sample is available for the specimen. Microscopic examination of the white powder fraction indicated pure calcite. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.coarse.calcit2.spectrum.txt

The sample was a light tan irregular mass and appeared to be a pure carbonate, both in hand specimen and under the microscope. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.coarse.ceruss1.spectrum.txt

The sample was a single white crystal, 3 cm x 2 cm x 2 cm and 29 g, showing good cleavage. No impurities or alteration were visible in hand sample. Under the microscope it appears very pure and unaltered.Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.coarse.dolomi1.spectrum.txt

The sample appeared to be pure white dolomite in hand specimen and under the microscope. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.coarse.dolomi2.spectrum.txt

The hand sample was massive, multicrystalline, and pink in color and appeared to be pure dolomite. Microscopic examination confirmed this. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.coarse.dolomi3.spectrum.txt

The sample was a large piece of a transparent single crystal and appeared to be pure fluorite. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.halide.none.coarse.flouri1.spectrum.txt

The sample consisted of a 4 cm x 2 cm x 2 cm cluster of light brown radiating acicular crystals weighing 10 g. No impurities were observed in hand sample or under the microscope. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers.(Goethite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.hydroxide.none.coarse.goethi1.spectrum.txt

The sample was a 2 cm x 2 cm x 2 cm layered botryoidal fragment weighing 14.5 g. Its surface was glossy black, with interior layers various shades of yellow-brown. No impurities were noted in hand sample or microscopically. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers.(Goethite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.hydroxide.none.coarse.goethi2.spectrum.txt

The sample was one large (3.5 cm x 2 cm x 2 cm) piece with a few crystal faces showing, weighing 42 g. Minimal contamination (<1%) occurred along a few thin veins containing mostly kaolinte. Microscopic examination confirmed the substantial purity of the sample. Most of the contaminating earthy material was removed by ultrasonic cleaning of the crushed sample. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers.(Spinel Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.oxide.none.coarse.chromi1.spectrum.txt

The sample was a 2.5 cm x 2.5 cm x 1 cm sawn plate, deep red in color and weighing 15.3 g. It appears clean with no impurities in hand sample or microscopically. Particle size was 74-250 micrometers.(Hematite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.oxide.none.coarse.hemati1.spectrum.txt

The sample consisted of two large and several smaller fragments. Each large piece was about 1 cm x 3 cm, with a total weight of 19 g. Many surfaces of this black mineral had a yellow-brown alteration coating, which was difficult to remove completely. Under the microscope the sample appeared pure. Particle size was 74-250 micrometers.(Hematite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.oxide.none.coarse.ilmeni1.spectrum.txt

The sample was composed of two large (3 cm x 3 cm x 2 cm) fragments of a single crystal, weighing 26.7 g. The crystal fragments were dark gray in color, with good crystal faces, all of which had a red-brown, earthy material adhering in places. These coatings were removed by ultrasonic cleaning. Particle size was 75-250 micrometers.(Spinel Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.oxide.none.coarse.magnet1.spectrum.txt

The sample was composed of three small crystals with a total weight of 3.83 g. Some reddish-brown clay mineral and muscovite coating one surface was scraped away. Veining inside crystals showed limonitic staining. The crushed sample was given an HCl and HF bath to remove stain, after which microscopic examination indicated a pure sample. Particle size was 75 - 250 Micrometers. (Rutile Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.oxide.none.coarse.rutile1.spectrum.txt

The sample consisted of two large and several smaller fragments. Each large piece was about 1 cm x 3 cm, with a total weight of 19 g. Many surfaces of this black mineral had a yellow-brown alteration coating, which was difficult to remove completely. Under the microscope the sample appeared pure. Sample had fracture surface.(Hematite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.oxide.none.solid.ilmeni1.spectrum.txt

The hand sample was cut and polished along an unknown axis. It was pale green and appeared pure. Microscopic examination showed a small amount (2% or less) of very fine, low birefringent material in the sample. The contaminating grains occasionally have rhombic or cubic shape. These grains were unidentifiable. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers. (Ring Silicates) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.cyclosilicate.coarse.beryl1.spectrum.txt

Hand sample was pale green to white in color and appeared pure on the inside, with a small amount of surface discoloration. The sample was roughly cubical, 2 cm on a side. Microscopic examination indicated it to be pure with a very small amount (much less than 1%) of alteration. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometer.(Ring Silicates) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.cyclosilicate.coarse.beryl2.spectrum.txt

The sample consists of three pieces, each about 2 cm x 4 cm x 1 cm. There are some heterogeneities in color, but the samples are predominantly blue-gray. They appear fairly pure, but do have a few veins with discoloration along them. Under the petrographic microscope all samples appear to be cordierite, but grains have a small amount of alteration and some microcrystalline inclusions. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers.(Ring Silicates) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.cyclosilicate.coarse.cordie2.spectrum.txt

The sample was a purple, translucent, sudhedral crystal, with some green coloration at one end. The crystal was 5 mm x 2 cm x 2 cm in size. Microscopic examination indicated that the mineral was pure.Particle size was 75 - 250 Micrometers.(Ring Silicates) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.cyclosilicate.coarse.tourma1.spectrum.txt
